mission.title: Install llm-d Tiered Prefix Cache Offloading
mission.description: Configure prefix cache offloading for llm-d deployments to extend cache capacity beyond GPU HBM. Supports multiple storage tiers: CPU RAM (recommended first tier), local disk, shared/remote storage (CephFS, GCP Lustre, IBM Storage Scale), and P2P cache sharing. Tiered caching avoids recomputation of KV tensors and SSM states, improving TTFT and throughput while reducing cost.

steps.title: Deploy a base llm-d stack
steps.description:
Tiered prefix cache is an addon to existing llm-d deployments. First deploy one of the well-lit paths:
- [Inference Scheduling](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/inference-scheduling)
- [P/D Disaggregation](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/pd-disaggregation)
- [Precise Prefix Cache Routing](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/precise-prefix-cache-aware)

steps.title: Enable CPU RAM offloading (recommended first tier)
steps.description:
CPU RAM offloading is recommended as the first cache tier because:
- Little operational overhead
- Much larger capacity than GPU HBM
- CPU-to-accelerator transfer is faster than recomputation

Follow the [CPU offloading guide](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/tiered-prefix-cache/cpu) to configure CPU RAM as a cache tier in your existing helmfile values.

steps.title: Configure local disk offloading (optional second tier)
steps.description:
Local disk increases cache capacity but is slower than CPU RAM. Consider this when:
- Your workload can tolerate additional latency
- Local disk capacity is sufficient

Follow the [storage offloading guide](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/tiered-prefix-cache/storage) to configure local disk as a storage backend.

steps.title: Configure shared storage offloading (optional tier)
steps.description:
Shared storage provides:
- Extended capacity independent of deployment size
- Cross-node cache sharing between replicas
- Fast scale-up (new nodes reuse existing cache)
- Persistence across restarts and failures

Supported storage systems: CephFS, GCP Lustre, IBM Storage Scale, and any vLLM-compatible storage connector.

Follow the [storage offloading guide](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/tiered-prefix-cache/storage).

steps.title: Apply configuration changes
steps.description:
After updating your helmfile values with cache tier configuration, re-apply:
```bash
helmfile apply -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

The cache tiers are configured in the model service values and take effect on pod restart.

steps.title: Verify cache offloading
steps.description:
Check model service pod logs to verify cache offloading is active:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/component=modelservice -n ${NAMESPACE} --tail=100 | grep -i cache
```

Monitor cache hit rates through the monitoring stack to validate improved performance.

resolution.summary: Successful configuration shows cache offloading active in model service logs. Recommended tier order: HBM (always) > CPU RAM > local disk or shared storage. Multiple tiers can be combined for optimal cache performance.

uninstall.title: Remove cache tier configuration
uninstall.description:
Edit your helmfile values to remove or disable cache offloading settings, then re-apply:
```bash
helmfile apply -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

Pods will restart without cache offloading enabled.

uninstall.title: Clean up shared storage resources

upgrade.title: Update cache configuration
upgrade.description: Modify cache tier settings in your helmfile values (e.g., increase CPU RAM allocation, add new storage tier).

upgrade.title: Re-apply with updated configuration
upgrade.description:
```bash
helmfile apply -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

Monitor cache performance after changes to validate improvement.

troubleshooting.title: CPU offloading not activating
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the CPU offloading configuration is correctly set in model service values. Check that sufficient CPU RAM is available on the nodes:
```bash
kubectl describe node <node-name> | grep -A 5 memory
```

troubleshooting.title: Shared storage latency too high
troubleshooting.description:
If shared storage adds too much latency, consider:
1. Using a faster storage backend (NVMe-backed, RDMA-capable)
2. Increasing the CPU RAM tier size to reduce shared storage access frequency
3. Enabling GPU Direct Storage (GDS) if supported by your storage system

troubleshooting.title: Cache hit rate low despite offloading
troubleshooting.description:
Low hit rates may indicate:
- Cache capacity is still too small for your workload
- Workload has low prefix overlap between requests
- Consider enabling [precise prefix cache routing](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/precise-prefix-cache-aware) for better cache-aware scheduling

prerequisites.description: An existing llm-d deployment (any well-lit path). For CPU offloading: nodes with sufficient CPU RAM. For shared storage: a compatible storage system (CephFS, GCP Lustre, IBM Storage Scale, etc.).
